key_insights.insight: True negotiation involves understanding the other person and knowing your fallback position.
key_insights.quote: Before I walk into a meeting with somebody, number one, try to know the personality of that person, know if there's a fallback position, if I'm not winning, what I'll accept.

key_insights.insight: Sometimes, being the smartest person in the room means letting others talk first to gather information.
key_insights.quote: Sometimes you can walk into a room and be the smartest person in the room. You don't want anybody to know that...Let them just talk.

key_insights.insight: Taking responsibility, even when it isn't entirely your fault, helps avoid repeating mistakes.
key_insights.quote: Anything that goes wrong, I take responsibility for personally. I should have known better...I always try to take responsibility so I don't make the same mistake again.

key_insights.insight: The government should not break the law to uphold the law because it sets a precedent for abuse.
key_insights.quote: The legally just side of me says that the government is never allowed to break the law, to uphold the law. Because if you allow that to happen, then it falls into monarchy and people are not protected.

key_insights.insight: Having your personal life in order helps drive business success because they affect one another.
key_insights.quote: Get your personal life in order because normally your business is going to be a reflection of your personal life in some way, shape, or form.

key_insights.insight: Even when facing immense pressure, it's vital to maintain relationships and contact with family to maintain your values.
key_insights.quote: When I went away, my fear was that was going to happen to me and my wife. So I did all I wanted. I told her, I told her, you want to lock me up? Lock me up. Just give me my phone calls and give me my visits.

key_insights.insight: There's a critical importance to do what you do best, delegate the rest, but you must also motivate them to the point where you're getting the most out of them. That's what leadership is about
key_insights.quote: Do what I do best, delegate the rest. And then hopefully you have the talent to motivate the people and get the most out of them. So it's a double sword, double-edged sword.

actionable_advice.advice: Implement 'sit-downs' for resolving business disputes, a structured meeting with clear rules and a final decision-maker.
actionable_advice.how_to: Structure meetings with an authority figure (like a boss) who makes a final, unappealable decision. Enforce respectful communication; disrespect leads to automatic loss of the argument.

actionable_advice.advice: Improve negotiation skills by observing and understanding the other party before revealing your own intentions or position.
actionable_advice.how_to: Listen more than you speak and try to learn the other person's personality and fallback position. Be the last person to talk to gather the most information.

actionable_advice.advice: Minimize micromanagement by delegating tasks to talented individuals and focusing on your own strengths.
actionable_advice.how_to: Identify your core competencies and delegate other tasks. Motivate your team by giving them autonomy and trust.

memorable_stories: Michael's first viewing of his friend Artie's body after he was murdered.
The story of how the mafia profited from the prohibition.
The story of his partner and the $33 million in the Swiss bank account.
Michael's description of the oath ceremony to become a 'made' man.
contrarian_claims: The US Government helped make the Mafia strong via Prohibition.
The government should never violate the law to uphold the law even when it is chasing someone.
Everyone has the potential to do damage if triggered correctly
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Former Mafia Boss: "I Was Running The Biggest Scam In The World! $1.4 Million A Day!" - Michael Franzese
